Code fragment: public final TableField<LinkRecord, OffsetDateTime> CHECKED_AT = createField(DSL.name("CHECKED_AT"), SQLDataType.TIMESTAMPWITHTIMEZONE(6).defaultValue(DSL.field(DSL.raw("'1970-01-01 00:00:00+00'"), SQLDataType.TIMESTAMPWITHTIMEZONE)), this, "");
Explanation: The CHECKED_AT column uses a special default value ('1970-01-01 00:00:00+00' - Unix epoch) to represent an unchecked state instead of using NULL. This magic value approach can lead to confusion and bugs. Consider using NULL to represent 'never checked' and adding .nullable(true) explicitly, or if the column should never be null, add .nullable(false).
